text: A learning-oriented walkthrough of CI/CD for the orch-studio repo, centered on how a push to `main` on GitHub could trigger an AWS production deployment. Claude explained GitHub Actions end to end â€” how workflow YAML in `.github/workflows/` maps to runnable steps, the distinction between `run` (arbitrary shell commands on the runner) and `uses` (prebuilt actions as reusable building blocks), and sketched the "protein" of a real deploy workflow for the Orchestrator Studio site. The session then turned practical, setting up a simple proof-of-concept Actions workflow that emails the user on every push to main as a test, and ended by walking through what was needed to get that change committed and pushed to the remote.
